template: Borrow the core claim from a different concept in the same chapter.
Take a real contrast and reverse which side owns the property.
Insert or remove a single negation from the real definition.
Turn a limited claim into an absolute one.
Turn a broad claim into an artificially narrow one.
Attach the concept to the wrong philosopher or source figure.
Use an example that belongs to a different concept.
Splice the first half of one concept with the second half of another.
Swap a key adjective or noun for its antonym.
Move the concept into the wrong parent framework.
Keep the action but change what the action is trying to optimize.
Confuse whether the concept cares about methods or outcomes.
Put the right steps in the wrong order.
Remove a qualifier like only, merely, usually, or under certain conditions.
Rephrase a genuine strength as if it were a weakness.
